name: Jaipur

palette.name: Pink City Sandstone
palette.rationale: Soft pink sandstone is the central architectural cue for Jaipur's walled-city identity.

palette.name: Hawa Mahal Rose
palette.rationale: A deeper rose gives the palette a palace-facade accent inspired by Hawa Mahal and historic masonry.

palette.name: Amber Fort Gold
palette.rationale: Warm gold supports palace, fort, and royal-craft associations without claiming a fixed heritage color.

palette.name: Blue Pottery
palette.rationale: Saturated blue adds a craft accent tied to Jaipur's well-known pottery and decorative arts.

palette.name: Desert Limewash
palette.rationale: Light warm neutral balances pink walls with courtyards, limewash, and dry Rajasthan sunlight.

palette.name: Courtyard Green
palette.rationale: Muted green represents palace courtyards, garden planting, and relief from the walled city's warm stone.

palette.name: Bazaar Shadow
palette.rationale: Dark brown provides practical contrast for bazaars, carved wood, shaded arcades, and type use.

sources.title: Jaipur City, Rajasthan
sources.note: Used for walled-city, planning, architecture, and heritage context.

sources.title: Jaipur Tourism
sources.note: Used for official tourism context, Pink City framing, forts, palaces, and crafts.

notes: Complete starter record; all colors are editorial interpretations of architecture, craft, garden, and bazaar atmosphere.
editorialSummary: Jaipur's starter palette centers Pink City sandstone and palace rose, then adds fort gold, blue pottery, desert limewash, courtyard green, and bazaar shadow for a warm heritage design system.
